No Poverty.reason: The course's focus on Business Data Analytics, statistical testing, and regression models provides skills applicable to measuring and analyzing poverty indicators (Target 1.2). Core competence B also emphasizes understanding sustainability issues.
evidence: understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues

Zero Hunger.reason: Data analysis methods taught, such as time-series forecasting and statistical testing, are applicable to monitoring food markets, agricultural productivity, and supply chains (Targets 2.3, 2.c). Core competence B also applies.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course explicitly teaches 'statistical testing' and 'regression models,' which are fundamental tools for analyzing health data, trends, and risks, supporting targets like 3.d on managing health risks.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it directly provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and explicitly aims to 'equip students with essential skills in data analysis' for 'employment, decent jobs and entrepreneurship' (Target 4.4).

Gender Equality.reason: The data analysis skills (statistical testing, regression) taught in the course can be applied to measure and analyze gender disparities in various sectors, supporting targets like 5.1 and 5.a. Core competence B also applies.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to clean water and sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course teaches 'time-series forecasting,' a method directly applicable to analyzing energy consumption, production, and efficiency trends (Target 7.3).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course explicitly aims to 'equip students with essential skills in data analysis' for 'employment, decent jobs and entrepreneurship,' directly aligning with Targets 8.3 and 8.5.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's core topics include 'Machine Learning' and 'Business Data Analytics,' which are direct drivers of technological upgrading and innovation in industrial sectors (Target 9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course teaches 'statistical testing' and 'regression models,' which are essential tools for measuring and analyzing various forms of inequalities, such as income disparities (Targets 10.1, 10.2).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to sustainable cities and communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course's data analysis skills, combined with the 'sustainability issues' core competence, enable students to analyze resource use and integrate sustainability information, supporting sustainable practices (Target 12.6).

Climate Action.reason: The course teaches 'time-series forecasting' and 'regression models,' which are key methods for climate data analysis, impact modeling, and informing climate change mitigation and adaptation strategies (Target 13.3).

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to life below water.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly relates to life on land.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The data analysis skills taught can be applied to analyze institutional performance, identify inefficiencies, and promote transparency and accountability, supporting Target 16.6.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course directly builds 'statistical capacity' and skills in 'data analysis,' which is crucial for increasing the availability of high-quality, timely, and reliable data (Target 17.18).
